qa_pairs.question: How do I sign up for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To sign up, visit the register link on the CCDB portal (https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/account_application). You must accept certain policies and agreements to obtain an account, which are available for review on the Alliance website (https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies). Once your account is approved, you can always view your accepted agreements on CCDB by navigating to 'My Account --> Agreements'.

qa_pairs.question: What are the different types of roles available when applying for an Alliance account, and who is eligible for them?
qa_pairs.answer: There are two main types of roles: sponsor roles, often referred to as Primary Investigators (PIs), and sponsored roles. Only faculty members from Canadian post-secondary academic institutions eligible for CFI funding can be sponsors. Sponsored roles are further divided into internal (e.g., undergraduate, masters, doctoral students; postdoctoral fellows; researchers paid by the PI's institution; non-research staff) and external (collaborators from a different institution than the PI). Administrators who are not faculty but lead research projects should contact accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca for appropriate accommodations.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after I submit my application for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: After submitting your request, you will receive an email with a link to confirm the email address you provided. If you are a principal investigator (PI), your application will be approved by a Federation staff member. If you are applying for a sponsored role, your application will first need to be confirmed by the principal investigator you identified as your sponsor before it can be approved by a Federation staff member.
